heading: Commodity whistleblower incentives and protection

children.heading: Covered judicial or administrative action
children.label: 1 Covered judicial or administrative action
children.text: The term “covered judicial or administrative action” means any judicial or administrative action brought by the Commission under this chapter that results in monetary sanctions exceeding $1,000,000.

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 2 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund established under subsection (g).

children.label: A
children.text: any monies, including penalties, disgorgement, restitution, and interest ordered to be paid; and

children.label: B
children.text: any monies deposited into a disgorgement fund or other fund pursuant to , as a result of such action or any settlement of such action.section 7246(b) of title 15

children.label: A
children.text: is derived from the independent knowledge or analysis of a whistleblower;

children.label: B
children.text: is not known to the Commission from any other source, unless the whistleblower is the original source of the information; and

children.label: C
children.text: is not exclusively derived from an allegation made in a judicial or administrative hearing, in a governmental report, hearing, audit, or investigation, or from the news media, unless the whistleblower is a source of the information.

children.heading: Related action
children.label: 5 Related action
children.text: The term “related action”, when used with respect to any judicial or administrative action brought by the Commission under this chapter, means any judicial or administrative action brought by an entity described in subclauses (I) through (VI) of subsection (h)(2)(C) that is based upon the original information provided by a whistleblower pursuant to subsection (a) that led to the successful enforcement of the Commission action.

children.heading: Successful resolution
children.label: 6 Successful resolution
children.text: The term “successful resolution”, when used with respect to any judicial or administrative action brought by the Commission under this chapter, includes any settlement of such action.

children.heading: Whistleblower
children.label: 7 Whistleblower
children.text: The term “whistleblower” means any individual, or 2 or more individuals acting jointly, who provides information relating to a violation of this chapter to the Commission, in a manner established by rule or regulation by the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 10 percent, in total, of what has been collected of the monetary sanctions imposed in the action or related actions; and

children.label: B
children.text: not more than 30 percent, in total, of what has been collected of the monetary sanctions imposed in the action or related actions.

children.heading: Payment of awards
children.label: 2 Payment of awards
children.text: Any amount paid under paragraph (1) shall be paid from the Fund.

children.heading: Determination of amount of award; denial of award
children.label: c Determination of amount of award; denial of award

children.heading: Discretion
children.label: A Discretion
children.text: The determination of the amount of an award made under subsection (b) shall be in the discretion of the Commission.

children.label: I
children.text: the significance of the information provided by the whistleblower to the success of the covered judicial or administrative action;

children.label: II
children.text: the degree of assistance provided by the whistleblower and any legal representative of the whistleblower in a covered judicial or administrative action;

children.label: III
children.text: the programmatic interest of the Commission in deterring violations of the chapter (including regulations under the chapter) by making awards to whistleblowers who provide information that leads to the successful enforcement of such laws; and11So in original. Probably should be “this”.1

children.label: IV
children.text: such additional relevant factors as the Commission may establish by rule or regulation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not take into consideration the balance of the Fund.

children.label: i
children.text: a appropriate regulatory agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Department of Justice;

children.label: iii
children.text: a registered entity;

children.label: iv
children.text: a registered futures association;

children.label: v
children.text: a self-regulatory organization as defined in ; orsection 78c(a) of title 15

children.label: vi
children.text: a law enforcement organization;

children.label: B
children.text: to any whistleblower who is convicted of a criminal violation related to the judicial or administrative action for which the whistleblower otherwise could receive an award under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: to any whistleblower who submits information to the Commission that is based on the facts underlying the covered action submitted previously by another whistleblower;

children.label: D
children.text: to any whistleblower who fails to submit information to the Commission in such form as the Commission may, by rule or regulation, require.

children.heading: Permitted representation
children.label: 1 Permitted representation
children.text: Any whistleblower who makes a claim for an award under subsection (b) may be represented by counsel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any whistleblower who anonymously makes a claim for an award under subsection (b) shall be represented by counsel if the whistleblower submits the information upon which the claim is based.

children.heading: Disclosure of identity
children.label: B Disclosure of identity
children.text: Prior to the payment of an award, a whistleblower shall disclose the identity of the whistleblower and provide such other information as the Commission may require, directly or through counsel for the whistleblower.

children.heading: No contract necessary
children.label: e No contract necessary
children.text: No contract with the Commission is necessary for any whistleblower to receive an award under subsection (b), unless otherwise required by the Commission, by rule or regulation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any determination made under this section, including whether, to whom, or in what amount to make awards, shall be in the discretion of the Commission.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: 2 Appeals
children.text: Any determination described in paragraph (1) may be appealed to the appropriate court of appeals of the United States not more than 30 days after the determination is issued by the Commission.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 3 Review
children.text: The court shall review the determination made by the Commission in accordance with section 7064 of title 5.2So in original. Probably should be “section 706”.2

children.heading: Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund
children.label: g Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a revolving fund to be known as the “Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund”.

children.label: A
children.text: the payment of awards to whistleblowers as provided in subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: the funding of customer education initiatives designed to help customers protect themselves against fraud or other violations of this chapter, or the rules and regulations thereunder.

children.heading: Monetary sanctions
children.label: A Monetary sanctions
children.text: Any monetary sanctions collected by the Commission in any covered judicial or administrative action that is not otherwise distributed to victims of a violation of this chapter or the rules and regulations thereunder underlying such action, unless the balance of the Fund at the time the monetary judgment is collected exceeds $100,000,000.

children.heading: Additional amounts
children.label: B Additional amounts
children.text: If the amounts deposited into or credited to the Fund under subparagraph (A) are not sufficient to satisfy an award made under subsection (b), there shall be deposited into or credited to the Fund an amount equal to the unsatisfied portion of the award from any monetary sanction collected by the Commission in any judicial or administrative action brought by the Commission under this chapter that is based on information provided by a whistleblower.

children.heading: Investment income
children.label: C Investment income
children.text: All income from investments made under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Amounts in Fund may be invested
children.label: A Amounts in Fund may be invested
children.text: The Commission may request the Secretary of the Treasury to invest the portion of the Fund that is not, in the Commission’s judgment, required to meet the current needs of the Fund.

children.heading: Eligible investments
children.label: B Eligible investments
children.text: Investments shall be made by the Secretary of the Treasury in obligations of the United States or obligations that are guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States, with maturities suitable to the needs of the Fund as determined by the Commission.

children.heading: Interest and proceeds credited
children.label: C Interest and proceeds credited
children.text: The interest on, and the proceeds from the sale or redemption of, any obligations held in the Fund shall be credited to, and form a part of, the Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: the Commission’s whistleblower award program under this section, including a description of the number of awards granted and the types of cases in which awards were granted during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: customer education initiatives described in paragraph (2)(B) that were funded by the Fund during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: C
children.text: the balance of the Fund at the beginning of the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: D
children.text: the amounts deposited into or credited to the Fund during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: E
children.text: the amount of earnings on investments of amounts in the Fund during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: F
children.text: the amount paid from the Fund during the preceding fiscal year to whistleblowers pursuant to subsection (b);

children.label: G
children.text: the amount paid from the Fund during the preceding fiscal year for customer education initiatives described in paragraph (2)(B);

children.label: H
children.text: the balance of the Fund at the end of the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: I
children.text: a complete set of audited financial statements, including a balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow analysis.

children.label: i
children.text: in providing information to the Commission in accordance with subsection (b); or

children.label: ii
children.text: in assisting in any investigation or judicial or administrative action of the Commission based upon or related to such information.

children.heading: Cause of action
children.label: i Cause of action
children.text: An individual who alleges discharge or other discrimination in violation of subparagraph (A) may bring an action under this subsection in the appropriate district court of the United States for the relief provided in subparagraph (C), unless the individual who is alleging discharge or other discrimination in violation of subparagraph (A) is an employee of the Federal Government, in which case the individual shall only bring an action under .section 1221 of title 5

children.heading: Subpoenas
children.label: ii Subpoenas
children.text: A subpoena requiring the attendance of a witness at a trial or hearing conducted under this subsection may be served at any place in the United States.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: iii Statute of limitations
children.text: An action under this subsection may not be brought more than 2 years after the date on which the violation reported in subparagraph (A) is committed.

children.label: i
children.text: reinstatement with the same seniority status that the individual would have had, but for the discrimination;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of back pay otherwise owed to the individual, with interest; and

children.label: iii
children.text: compensation for any special damages sustained as a result of the discharge or discrimination, including litigation costs, expert witness fees, and reasonable attorney’s fees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), the Commission, and any officer or employee of the Commission, shall not disclose any information, including information provided by a whistleblower to the Commission, which could reasonably be expected to reveal the identity of a whistleblower, except in accordance with the provisions of , unless and until required to be disclosed to a defendant or respondent in connection with a public proceeding instituted by the Commission or any entity described in subparagraph (C). For purposes of , this paragraph shall be considered a statute described in subsection (b)(3)(B) of such section 552.section 552a of title 5section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Effect
children.label: B Effect
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph is intended to limit the ability of the Attorney General to present such evidence to a grand jury or to share such evidence with potential witnesses or defendants in the course of an ongoing criminal investigation.

children.label: I
children.text: the Department of Justice;

children.label: II
children.text: an appropriate department or agency of the Federal Government, acting within the scope of its jurisdiction;

children.label: III
children.text: a registered entity, registered futures association, or self-regulatory organization as defined in ;section 78c(a) of title 15

children.label: IV
children.text: a State attorney general in connection with any criminal investigation;

children.label: V
children.text: an appropriate department or agency of any State, acting within the scope of its jurisdiction; and

children.label: VI
children.text: a foreign futures authority.

children.heading: Maintenance of information
children.label: ii Maintenance of information
children.text: Each of the entities, agencies, or persons described in clause (i) shall maintain information described in that clause as confidential, in accordance with the requirements in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Study on impact of FOIA exemption on Commodity Futures Trading Commission
children.label: iii Study on impact of FOIA exemption on Commodity Futures Trading Commission
aa
children.text: on whether the exemption under (known as the Freedom of Information Act) established in paragraph (2)(A) aids whistleblowers in disclosing information to the Commission;section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.label: bb
children.text: on what impact the exemption has had on the public’s ability to access information about the Commission’s regulation of commodity futures and option markets; and

children.label: cc
children.text: to make any recommendations on whether the Commission should continue to use the exemption.

children.label: aa
children.text: submit a report on the findings of the study required under this clause to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: bb
children.text: make the report available to the public through publication of a report on the website of the Commission.

children.heading: Rights retained
children.label: 3 Rights retained
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be deemed to diminish the rights, privileges, or remedies of any whistleblower under any Federal or State law, or under any collective bargaining agreement.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority
children.label: i Rulemaking authority
children.text: The Commission shall have the authority to issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to implement the provisions of this section consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Implementing rules
children.label: j Implementing rules
children.text: The Commission shall issue final rules or regulations implementing the provisions of this section not later than 270 days after .July 21, 2010

children.heading: Original information
children.label: k Original information
children.text: Information submitted to the Commission by a whistleblower in accordance with rules or regulations implementing this section shall not lose its status as original information solely because the whistleblower submitted such information prior to the effective date of such rules or regulations, provided such information was submitted after .July 21, 2010

children.heading: Awards
children.label: l Awards
children.text: A whistleblower may receive an award pursuant to this section regardless of whether any violation of a provision of this chapter, or a rule or regulation thereunder, underlying the judicial or administrative action upon which the award is based occurred prior to .July 21, 2010

children.heading: Provision of false information
children.label: m Provision of false information
children.text: A whistleblower who knowingly and willfully makes any false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation, or who makes or uses any false writing or document knowing the same to contain any false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or entry, shall not be entitled to an award under this section and shall be subject to prosecution under .section 1001 of title 18

children.heading: Nonenforceability of certain provisions waiving rights and remedies or requiring arbitration of disputes
children.label: n Nonenforceability of certain provisions waiving rights and remedies or requiring arbitration of disputes

children.heading: Waiver of rights and remedies
children.label: 1 Waiver of rights and remedies
children.text: The rights and remedies provided for in this section may not be waived by any agreement, policy form, or condition of employment including by a predispute arbitration agreement.

children.heading: Predispute arbitration agreements
children.label: 2 Predispute arbitration agreements
children.text: No predispute arbitration agreement shall be valid or enforceable, if the agreement requires arbitration of a dispute arising under this section.
